sources.data_scope: 3M+ global platform users, 200M+ applications, 5.5M+ hires annually

sources.data_scope: Enterprise recruitment marketing platform data

sources.name: CareerPlug Recruiting Metrics & KPIs (2025)
sources.data_scope: Applicant-to-hire ratios, funnel benchmarks by industry

cpa.2025: $35.00 (Appcast 2026 report, highest CPA of any occupation)
cpa.notes: Frontline 'standing-up' roles remain costly and hard to fill. Healthcare is structurally constrained.

cph.notes: Second-highest CPH of any occupation after technology. Includes background checks, credential verification, drug testing, compliance measures

apply_rate.notes: Lowest apply rate of any sector. Ultra-tight labor market. Standing-up roles chronically hard to fill.

time_to_fill.notes: Twice the national average; longest of any major industry

quality_of_hire.notes: NSI 2025 National Health Care Retention Report

source_of_hire.notes: Heavy reliance on staffing agencies for specialized roles

seasonal_patterns.notes: Year-round demand with surges in Q1 and September. Vacation season causes summer dip. Healthcare accounted for 75% of net job growth in 2025.

cpa.2025: $14.78 (Appcast recruitment media CPA), $45-$89 (total cost including agency/ATS)
cpa.notes: Technology had highest apply rate (7.14%) in 2025 but CPH doubled to $2,795 — volume masks quality problem

cph.notes: Deloitte 2024 benchmark. Hidden costs include engineering team evaluation time.

apply_rate.2025: Rising further due to white-collar recession

time_to_fill.notes: Multiple technical interview rounds drive longer timelines. SmartRecruiters suggests 26% reduction possible by accelerating interview-to-offer stage.

source_of_hire.notes: Direct sourcing and referrals dominate quality hires in tech

seasonal_patterns.notes: AI/ML demand surged 25% in 2025 (CompTIA). Tech postings remain 30% below pre-pandemic despite stabilization.

cpa.notes: Finance CPAs relatively stable. Sitting-down occupation with higher apply rates.

cpc.notes: LinkedIn premium for financial services roles

cph.notes: Strict background checks, compliance requirements add to costs

apply_rate.notes: White-collar roles seeing elevated apply rates in softer market

time_to_fill.notes: CPA shortage driving longer fill times for credentialed positions

seasonal_patterns.cpa_seasonal_note: Tax season prep (Q4-Q1) creates biggest demand spike for accounting/finance roles
seasonal_patterns.notes: Budget cycles drive Q1 hiring. Tariff-driven volatility affecting 2025-2026 hiring patterns.

cpc.notes: Lowest CPC of major industries due to high candidate volume

cph.notes: High-volume, entry-level hiring keeps costs low

apply_rate.notes: Good apply rates but high abandonment for hourly roles

time_to_fill.notes: Fastest hiring cycle among major industries

seasonal_patterns.notes: 2025 seasonal retail postings were flat YoY (+1%), seasonal sales roles down 10% YoY. E-commerce shifting seasonal demand to logistics.

cpa.2025: $18 median; short-term easing but YoY still elevated

cpc.trend_yoy: Volatile (+53.2% MoM in July 2025 per Recruitics)

apply_rate.notes: Highest application abandonment and interview no-show rates of any industry (iCIMS)

time_to_fill.notes: Fast hiring cycles due to standardized requirements and high volume needs

seasonal_patterns.notes: Hospitality attraction prices dropped 39% MoM in March 2025 but were 41% higher YoY (Recruitics May 2025).

cpc.notes: Indeed and ZipRecruiter strongest platforms for logistics hiring

cph.notes: 76% of transport/logistics employers report difficulty filling roles

apply_rate.notes: Improved slightly as market softened but structural constraints persist

seasonal_patterns.holiday_logistics_surge: Driving jobs up 153% from mid-Nov levels; loading/stocking up 49%
seasonal_patterns.notes: E-commerce growth shifting seasonal demand from retail to logistics. 31% of companies paused hiring in 2025 but key logistics roles remain in demand.

cpa.notes: Among hardest-to-fill occupational categories. CPA above $15 but below $35 range for most roles (Appcast 2025).

cpc.notes: Indeed and niche job boards most effective for skilled trades

cph.notes: 439,000 new net skilled craft workers needed in 2025 to meet demand

apply_rate.notes: Standing-up jobs have lower apply rates than sitting-down jobs across the board

labor_market_context.apprentice_pipeline: 680,000 active registered apprentices in FY2024, up from 562K in 2020

seasonal_patterns.notes: Construction and outdoor trades peak in spring/summer. Infrastructure spending driving year-round demand.

cpa.notes: Among most expensive industries to recruit. Talent scarcity and specialized skill requirements drive premium.

cph.notes: Years of schooling and high-risk environment make compensation higher than most industries

apply_rate.notes: Specialized roles have lower apply rates; general pharma manufacturing better

seasonal_patterns.notes: Budget cycles and clinical trial timelines drive hiring patterns. Q1 and Q3 are strongest.

cpa.notes: Security clearance requirement drastically shrinks candidate pool. Only ~2M people hold active clearance (0.6% of population).

cph.notes: Hiring cited as top operational constraint by industry leaders in 2025 CNBC report

apply_rate.notes: Extremely limited candidate pool for cleared positions

time_to_fill.notes: Security clearance verification adds significant time

seasonal_patterns.notes: Government fiscal year (Oct-Sep) and defense budget cycles drive hiring. Q4 government fiscal year-end creates urgency.

cpa.notes: Safety credentials and specialized certifications add to recruitment complexity

cph.notes: Similar cost tier to healthcare and tech due to specialized, safety-credentialed workforce

seasonal_patterns.notes: Utility maintenance cycles and energy demand patterns influence hiring. Renewable energy sector growing rapidly.

cpa.notes: 439,000 new net skilled craft workers needed in 2025. Wages growing 4-5% annually.

cph.up_to_3_4x_salary_total_cost: For a $60K role, up to $180,000-$240,000 total investment

seasonal_patterns.notes: Spring ramp-up for construction season. Weather-dependent markets have more pronounced seasonality.

cph.notes: Constrained budgets; mission-driven attraction reduces some costs

apply_rate.notes: Education has relatively good apply rates but limited candidate pools for specialized subjects

seasonal_patterns.notes: Academic calendar drives hiring. Spring semester hiring for next academic year. Mid-year replacements are urgent fills.

cpa.notes: Structured pay scales and civil service processes. USAJOBS primary platform.

cph.notes: Budget rigidity and lengthy approval processes can drive up indirect costs

apply_rate.notes: Government roles attract steady interest; lowest turnover (quit rate 0.8%)

time_to_fill.notes: Bureaucratic processes and security clearance requirements extend timelines significantly

seasonal_patterns.notes: Federal fiscal year (Oct 1) drives hiring. End-of-fiscal-year budget spending creates Q4 surge. Hiring freezes common at administration changes.

cpa.notes: Slightly higher than finance. Apply rates declining (7.3% in 2025).

cpc.notes: Insurance PPC for customer acquisition is extremely expensive ($900-$1,100 CPC) but recruitment CPC is much lower

apply_rate.notes: Apply rates declined in 2025 as market tightened for insurance talent

seasonal_patterns.notes: Open enrollment periods and regulatory changes drive hiring cycles
5G rollout and infrastructure investment driving demand. Technology overlap means competition with pure tech companies for talent.

cph.notes: Technical assessments and specialized evaluations add significant cost

apply_rate.notes: Manufacturing openings up 2% YoY per iCIMS January 2026

seasonal_patterns.notes: Model year changeovers and production ramp-ups drive seasonal patterns. EV transition creating new talent needs.

apply_rate.notes: High volume of applications for hourly roles

seasonal_patterns.notes: Seasonal food production cycles and holiday preparation drive patterns. Food service wage growth (2.9% YoY to Dec 2025) lagging retail.

cph.notes: Wide variance between front-line entertainment and digital/tech roles

seasonal_patterns.notes: Media sector remains significantly weaker than pre-pandemic per Indeed. Content production cycles drive hiring.

cph.notes: Hiring costs can average $16,789 per legal hire

apply_rate.notes: Sitting-down occupation with moderate apply rates

seasonal_patterns.notes: Law firm budget cycles and associate class hiring timelines drive patterns. Summer associate programs are separate pipeline.

cph.notes: Professional services includes accounting, advertising, marketing, consulting

funnel_benchmarks.notes: Gem data: only 2-3% of applicants invited to interview for consultant/analyst roles

seasonal_patterns.notes: Campus recruiting cycles (fall) and project-driven hiring create bimodal pattern.

cpa.notes: Mission-driven attraction reduces some costs. Smaller budgets limit paid advertising.

apply_rate.notes: Good apply rates due to mission alignment. Smaller applicant pools overall.

time_to_fill.notes: Hiring typically takes ~30 days for nonprofits (faster than hospitals)

seasonal_patterns.notes: Grant cycles and fiscal year budgets drive hiring. Year-end fundraising season limits bandwidth for hiring.

apply_rate.notes: Rural locations and seasonal nature limit candidate pools

seasonal_patterns.notes: Strongly seasonal. Planting and harvest seasons drive bulk of hiring. H-2A visa program critical for seasonal labor.

job_ad_impression_to_click.notes: CTR varies significantly by platform, ad creative, and targeting

click_to_application_start.notes: Mobile now 67% of all applications (July 2025). Apply rates declined in 2025.

application_start_to_completion.overall_benchmark: 50%+ is healthy; below 30% needs immediate attention

application_to_screening.notes: Varies widely. Some ATS auto-screen out 60-75% of applicants.

screening_to_interview.notes: 75% of candidates screened out at resume review stage in top firms

offer_to_acceptance.below_60_pct: Indicates serious compensation or process issues
offer_to_acceptance.notes: Candidates 12% less likely to reject offers in 2025 vs 2023 (Glassdoor). Trend toward higher acceptance as market softens.

overall_applicant_to_hire.gem_2026_report: 0.5% receive offers; 0.3-0.7% hired depending on company size

direct_sourcing_outbound.conversion_multiplier: 5-8x more likely to be hired than inbound
direct_sourcing_outbound.notes: 4x yield ratio. Sourced candidates are the most efficient hire source.

rediscovered_crm_ats.trend: Rising rapidly; talent rediscovery is a major efficiency lever

career_site.notes: Strong inbound channel when employer brand is strong

social_media.notes: LinkedIn dominates; organizations using LinkedIn 8x more likely to hire well than job boards only

agencies_third_party.notes: Usage increasing when internal channels underperform. Fees 15-30% of first-year salary.

alumni_networks_career_centers.notes: Growing channel, especially for reaching younger generations

small_companies.notes: Advance more candidates early but filter aggressively later

large_companies.notes: Fewer advance early but stronger later-stage conversion. Double the hire rate of smaller companies.

remote.notes: Remote roles attract dramatically more applicants but increase screening burden

hybrid.notes: Dominant model emerging. Balances cost efficiency with collaboration.

onsite.higher_cpa: +34% vs remote in markets with <3.5% unemployment
onsite.notes: Facing higher CPAs and slower fill times, especially in tight labor markets

salary_transparency_impact.notes: Salary disclosure significantly improves apply rates regardless of work model

women.lower_early_stage: Lower passthrough at top of funnel
women.notes: Gem data: Despite imbalance in outreach, women have higher end-to-end passthrough rates

market_overview.notes: Only 34% of enterprise companies use programmatic job advertising despite proven ROI

performance_vs_manual.pandologic_case_study: 472% increase in applicant volume with 533% cost decrease (Dominos)
performance_vs_manual.ai_automation_reduction: Up to 75% reduction in hiring costs, 71% decrease in CPH for AI-based recruitment automation

rtb_real_time_bidding.optimization_gap: 37.8% of open web ad spending goes to non-quality impressions
rtb_real_time_bidding.notes: Programmatic direct and preferred deals offer price certainty. Open exchange RTB offers scale and dynamic pricing.

cpc.best_for: Volume-focused campaigns, driving career site traffic
cpc.notes: Most common model for recruitment advertising

cpm.notes: Less common for direct recruitment, more for awareness

cpqa.trend: Emerging as preferred metric over CPA
cpqa.rationale: AI-generated applications inflating CPA metrics with low-quality resumes
cpqa.notes: Measures cost of applications meeting predefined qualification standards

budget_allocation_best_practices.dynamic_reallocation: Shift budget from filled roles to hard-to-fill automatically
budget_allocation_best_practices.critical_roles_priority: Fully fund critical openings rather than spreading thin

recommended_split.notes: Balance depends on role type, urgency, and budget. Programmatic recommended for volume; direct for niche/premium.

publisher_performance.notes: Publisher performance data varies by role type and geography. Key insight: platforms continuously analyze performance across 100+ sites and reallocate budget in real time to channels delivering the most qualified traffic.
publisher_performance.indeed: Best for volume/hourly hiring, strong apply rates
publisher_performance.linkedin: Best for professional/senior roles, highest CPC but strongest targeting
publisher_performance.ziprecruiter: Good for high-volume hiring, subscription model
publisher_performance.glassdoor: Strong employer branding, bundled with Indeed
publisher_performance.google_for_jobs: Growing organic channel, no direct advertising cost
publisher_performance.niche_job_boards: Best for specialized roles (Dice for tech, Health eCareers for healthcare, ClearanceJobs for defense)

january_february.description: New budgets, highest job postings, strong candidate engagement. Hiring spree from backlog of roles and new annual goals.
january_february.recommended_action: Maximize budget allocation. Launch major campaigns.

march_april_may.description: Continued momentum. Spring construction/outdoor trades ramp up. Hospitality begins seasonal hiring.
march_april_may.recommended_action: Maintain strong spend. Target seasonal industries.

june_july_august.description: Summer slowdown. PTO schedules, reduced availability. Stalled decisions.
june_july_august.recommended_action: Lower CPA bids may be possible. Good time for employer branding.

september_october.description: Post-Labor Day hiring push. Companies rush to fill before year-end. Federal fiscal year-end spending.
september_october.recommended_action: Increase budget. CPAs tend to rise with competition.

november_december.description: Holiday retail/logistics surge. White-collar hiring slows. Year-end projects and budget planning.
november_december.recommended_action: Focus on seasonal hiring (logistics +153%, loading +49%). Reduce white-collar spend.

healthcare.cpa_variance: +20% above average in peak; -10% in trough

technology.cpa_variance: +15% in Q1 peak; stable in Q3
technology.notes: Less seasonal than other industries but follows budget cycles

retail.notes: 2025 seasonal retail flat (+1% YoY); demand shifting to logistics

hospitality.cpa_variance: +50% variance between peak and trough (Recruitics data)

construction.notes: Strongly weather-dependent; Sun Belt less seasonal

logistics.notes: E-commerce driving logistics demand; seasonal driving jobs up 153%

education.notes: Academic calendar driven. Spring hiring for next academic year.

government.notes: Federal fiscal year (Oct 1) drives end-of-year spending and hiring urgency

agriculture.notes: Strongly seasonal: planting (spring) and harvest (fall) create bimodal peaks

q1_jan_mar.reason: High competition from new budgets, January hiring surge
q1_jan_mar.recommendation: Budget heavily but optimize bids carefully

q2_apr_jun.recommendation: Good value period for proactive hiring

q3_jul_sep.reason: Summer trough gives way to September Surge
q3_jul_sep.recommendation: Take advantage of July-August dip, prepare for September rise

q4_oct_dec.reason: Year-end push followed by holiday slowdown
q4_oct_dec.recommendation: Front-load October hiring; CPAs may soften in December

indeed.free_postings: Up to 3 free job postings/month (30 days, lower visibility)
indeed.notes: CPC varies heavily by industry, seniority, geography. Partnership with Glassdoor bundles postings.

ziprecruiter.matching_efficiency: 80% of employers receive quality candidate in first 24 hours

glassdoor.employer_branding_value: Strong; Glassdoor ratings improve apply rates
glassdoor.notes: Sponsoring a job on Indeed now gets posted on Glassdoor too

google_ads_for_recruitment.notes: Google for Jobs (organic) is free and growing. Paid Google Ads can target recruitment keywords.

recruitics.notes: Talent Market Index provides monthly pricing benchmarks across 9 sectors

where_candidates_drop_off.notes: 33% of companies lack confidence in interviewing capabilities; 50% believe they've lost hires to poor interviews

ai_generated_applications.trend: Inflating application volumes with generic, poorly matched resumes
ai_generated_applications.impact: Inflates CPA metrics while offering little hiring value
ai_generated_applications.response: Shift to CPQA (Cost Per Qualified Application) as primary metric

ai_and_technology.pct_planning_ai_investment: 84% say improving recruiter efficiency through AI is top priority
ai_and_technology.pct_using_ai_screening: 25% of hiring managers use AI to screen

macro_trends.the_great_stay: Quits, hires, and layoffs all declining. Employees staying put.
macro_trends.the_great_freeze: Employee turnover dropped from 177% (2023) to 50% (2025) - historic low
macro_trends.white_collar_recession: Tech, media, professional services postings well below pre-pandemic. Apply rates rising as supply exceeds demand.
macro_trends.healthcare_dominance: Healthcare accounted for 75% of net job growth in 2025

employer_sentiment.recruiter_stress: 54% found job more stressful than prior year

compensation_trends.signing_bonuses_declining: Only 15.4% received in Q4 2025 (down from 21.7% Q3)
compensation_trends.offer_rejection_rate_decline: 12% less likely to reject in 2025 vs 2023

recruitics_talent_market_index_monthly.description: Monthly talent attraction pricing data across 9 job sectors from Recruitics
